basic_details.job_title: GSSSB Municipal Sanitary Inspector Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 173 Posts
basic_details.total_vacancies: Municipal Sanitary Inspector, Class-3; Sanitary Sub Inspector, Class-3
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Single-stage CBRT/OMR MCQ exam (Part A + Part B)
basic_details.short_information: Gujarat Gauna Seva Pasandagi Mandal (GSSSB), Gandhinagar, has invited online applications for Municipal Sanitary Inspector and Sanitary Sub Inspector, Class-3 posts under Advertisement No. 349/202526. Following a corrigendum, the total number of vacancies has been increased from 75 to 173, and the online application window has been reopened from 20 July 2026 to 26 July 2026. This article covers complete details on eligibility, age limit, salary, application fee, selection process, and how to apply.

important_dates.raw: Original notification published: 26/08/2025 Corrigendum issued: 20/07/2026 Revised online application start: 20/07/2026, 2:00 PM Revised online application end: 26/07/2026, 11:59 PM Revised fee payment last date: 29/07/2026, 11:59 PM Age limit cut-off date: 15/09/2025 Educational qualification cut-off date: 26/07/2026 (revised from 15/09/2025)

application_fee.details: Fee must be paid online only (Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallets); a transaction fee applies in addition. Fee is refundable to candidates who appear for the examination; not refundable for those who do not appear. Revised last date to pay the fee: 29/07/2026, 11:59 PM.

age_limit.details: As on 15/09/2025, the candidate's age must not be less than 18 years and not more than 33 years. Upper age limit may be relaxed for candidates already in Gujarat Government service, as per the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967.

qualification.education_qualification: HSC + Sanitary Inspector Diploma / ITI Health Sanitary Inspector Certificate
qualification.details: The candidate must be a citizen of India, or possess nationality as per Rule-7 of the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. | Passed the Higher Secondary School Certificate Examination from a Secondary/Higher Secondary Education Board, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Government; and A minimum one-year Sanitary Inspector's Diploma Course from a Government-recognized institution; or A minimum one-year Certificate of Health Sanitary Inspectors from an Industrial Training Institute (ITI) or any other Government-recognized institute; and Basic knowledge of Computer Application as prescribed under the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967; and Adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi, or both. | Candidates without a basic computer knowledge certificate at the time of application may still apply, but must submit proof of having passed a basic computer knowledge test before appointment, failing which their selection will be cancelled.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be appointed on a fixed monthly pay of ₹26,000 for the first five years, along with other benefits as per applicable Finance Department resolutions. After five years, subject to satisfactory service, candidates become eligible for regular appointment in the 7th Pay Commission scale of ₹25,000 to ₹81,100 (Level-4), subject to the outcome of Supreme Court SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012. For candidates appointed at Gandhinagar Municipal Corporation specifically: they will not be treated as Gujarat Government employees, and after three years of satisfactory service become eligible for the same 7th Pay Commission Level-4 scale (₹25,500–₹81,100 per the corrigendum).

selection_process: Part B Indian Constitution, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English Comprehension 30
Selection is through a single-stage competitive exam conducted in CBRT/OMR (Computer Based Response Test / Optical Mark Recognition) mode
Combined duration for Part A and Part B: 3 hours (180 minutes). Negative marking: 1/4 mark deducted per wrong answer. Qualifying standard: minimum 40% in Part A and minimum 40% in Part B
for all categories. Candidates scoring the required qualifying marks will be shortlisted for document verification
called at approximately twice the number of category-wise vacancies. Final selection/waiting list will be prepared based on merit within the category-wise vacancies.
how_to_apply: Visit https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in and click "Online Application" → "Apply", then select GSSSB.
Select Advertisement No. 349/202526 (Municipal Sanitary Inspector, Class-3) and click Apply.
Click "Apply Now", then "Skip" to open the application format; fill in Personal Details (fields marked with a red asterisk are mandatory).
Accept the declaration ("Assurance") and click Save to generate your Application Number.
Upload your photograph (5 cm height × 3.6 cm width) and signature (2.5 cm height × 7.5 cm width) in JPG format, each under 15 KB.
Click "Confirm Application" - once confirmed, no further edits are possible.
Note the Confirmation Number generated; this is required for all further steps.
Download and print a copy of the application form for your records.
Pay the applicable examination fee online by 29/07/2026 (11:59 PM) via Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallet, and save the payment receipt.

Candidates should submit only one application. If multiple applications are submitted, the last confirmed application (with fee paid) will be considered valid, and earlier ones will be cancelled; fees paid on cancelled applications will not be refunded or adjusted.
important_instructions: Category (caste) details cannot be changed once the application is confirmed. Details filled in the application will be treated as final for the entire recruitment process and must be supported by original certificates at the time of verification. Foreign university degree holders must submit UGC equivalence proof at the time of certificate verification. The same photograph must be used throughout the recruitment process (exam attendance sheet, certificate verification, etc.). Keep the mobile number and email ID provided in the application active throughout the recruitment process, as SMS alerts will be sent. No waiting list will be prepared, as per General Administration Department resolution dated 27/07/2018. Malpractice, impersonation, or use of unfair means during the exam is punishable under the Gujarat Public Examination (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2023, in addition to disqualification. Scribe/compensatory time facility (20 minutes per hour) is available for eligible PwD candidates on request, submitted at least two days before the exam via email to [email protected]. For guidance on eligibility, application, or fee payment, candidates may contact the Mandal's office at 079-23258916.

content_sections.heading: GSSSB Sanitary Inspector Recruitment 2026: Quick Overview

rows.details: Municipal Sanitary Inspector, Class-3; Sanitary Sub Inspector, Class-3
HSC + Sanitary Inspector Diploma / ITI Health Sanitary Inspector Certificate
Single-stage CBRT/OMR MCQ exam (Part A + Part B)

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Original notification published: 26/08/2025 Corrigendum issued: 20/07/2026 Revised online application start: 20/07/2026, 2:00 PM Revised online application end: 26/07/2026, 11:59 PM Revised fee payment last date: 29/07/2026, 11:59 PM Age limit cut-off date: 15/09/2025 Educational qualification cut-off date: 26/07/2026 (revised from 15/09/2025)

content_sections.heading: GSSSB Sanitary Inspector Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: Note: The category-wise breakup above reflects the revised (corrigendum) figures. Reserved vacancies are applicable only to candidates who are original residents of Gujarat belonging to the respective reserved category.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: The candidate must be a citizen of India, or possess nationality as per Rule-7 of the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. Passed the Higher Secondary School Certificate Examination from a Secondary/Higher Secondary Education Board, or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Government; and A minimum one-year Sanitary Inspector's Diploma Course from a Government-recognized institution; or A minimum one-year Certificate of Health Sanitary Inspectors from an Industrial Training Institute (ITI) or any other Government-recognized institute; and Basic knowledge of Computer Application as prescribed under the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967; and Adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi, or both. Candidates without a basic computer knowledge certificate at the time of application may still apply, but must submit proof of having passed a basic computer knowledge test before appointment, failing which their selection will be cancelled.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: As on 15/09/2025, the candidate's age must not be less than 18 years and not more than 33 years. Upper age limit may be relaxed for candidates already in Gujarat Government service, as per the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967.

content_sections.heading: Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be appointed on a fixed monthly pay of ₹26,000 for the first five years, along with other benefits as per applicable Finance Department resolutions. After five years, subject to satisfactory service, candidates become eligible for regular appointment in the 7th Pay Commission scale of ₹25,000 to ₹81,100 (Level-4) , subject to the outcome of Supreme Court SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012. For candidates appointed at Gandhinagar Municipal Corporation specifically: they will not be treated as Gujarat Government employees, and after three years of satisfactory service become eligible for the same 7th Pay Commission Level-4 scale (₹25,500–₹81,100 per the corrigendum).

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Fee must be paid online only (Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallets); a transaction fee applies in addition. Fee is refundable to candidates who appear for the examination; not refundable for those who do not appear. Revised last date to pay the fee: 29/07/2026, 11:59 PM .

rows.category: Reserved (Women - all categories, SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, PwD, Ex-servicemen)

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is through a single-stage competitive exam conducted in CBRT/OMR (Computer Based Response Test / Optical Mark Recognition) mode, comprising objective MCQ-type questions, divided into two parts: Combined duration for Part A and Part B: 3 hours (180 minutes). Negative marking: 1/4 mark deducted per wrong answer. Qualifying standard: minimum 40% in Part A and minimum 40% in Part B, independently, for all categories. Candidates scoring the required qualifying marks will be shortlisted for document verification, called at approximately twice the number of category-wise vacancies. Final selection/waiting list will be prepared based on merit within the category-wise vacancies.

rows.subject: Indian Constitution, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English Comprehension

content_sections.heading: Additional Merit Benefits
content_sections.text: Widow candidates: 5% additional marks on the exam score, provided they have not remarried at the time of appointment. Sportspersons: 5% additional marks for candidates who represented at national/international, inter-university, or All India School Federation level in specified sports (athletics, badminton, basketball, cricket, football, hockey, swimming, table tennis, volleyball, tennis, weightlifting, wrestling, boxing, cycling, gymnastics, judo, rifle shooting, kabaddi, kho-kho, archery, equestrian, shot put, rowing, chess, handball).

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Educational qualification certificates (HSC + Sanitary Inspector Diploma / ITI Health Sanitary Inspector Certificate) Birth certificate / School Leaving Certificate for age proof Caste/category certificate (SC/ST/SEBC/EWS), issued by a competent authority in the prescribed format, where applicable Non-creamy layer certificate (for SEBC candidates) or Income and Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates) - see certificate timelines below Disability certificate (for PwD candidates, where applicable) Ex-servicemen identity card and discharge book (where applicable) Basic computer knowledge certificate (or proof of passing before appointment) Recent passport-size photograph (white background, dated within 1 year of the last application date) and signature, in the prescribed dimensions Certificate in Gujarati format (Annexure-4), based on income for financial year 2023-24/2024-25/2025-26, obtained on or before 26/07/2026 - or a certificate in Annexure-A format under Government of India provisions, obtained between 01/04/2026 and 26/07/2026. Age relaxation applies only if one of these is submitted. Certificate in Annexure-KH (English) or Parishisht-C (Gujarati) format, obtained between 27/07/2023 and 26/07/2026 - or a certificate based on financial year 2024-25 income, obtained between 01/04/2026 and 26/07/2026. Certificates issued for Gujarat state scheme benefits will not be accepted for this purpose.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Candidates should submit only one application. If multiple applications are submitted, the last confirmed application (with fee paid) will be considered valid, and earlier ones will be cancelled; fees paid on cancelled applications will not be refunded or adjusted.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Category (caste) details cannot be changed once the application is confirmed. Details filled in the application will be treated as final for the entire recruitment process and must be supported by original certificates at the time of verification. Foreign university degree holders must submit UGC equivalence proof at the time of certificate verification. The same photograph must be used throughout the recruitment process (exam attendance sheet, certificate verification, etc.). Keep the mobile number and email ID provided in the application active throughout the recruitment process, as SMS alerts will be sent. No waiting list will be prepared, as per General Administration Department resolution dated 27/07/2018. Malpractice, impersonation, or use of unfair means during the exam is punishable under the Gujarat Public Examination (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2023, in addition to disqualification. Scribe/compensatory time facility (20 minutes per hour) is available for eligible PwD candidates on request, submitted at least two days before the exam via email to [email protected] . For guidance on eligibility, application, or fee payment, candidates may contact the Mandal's office at 079-23258916.

faq.question: Q1. How many total vacancies are there in GSSSB Advertisement No. 349/202526?
faq.answer: 173 vacancies in total - 145 for Municipal Sanitary Inspector, Class-3, and 28 for Sanitary Sub Inspector, Class-3 at Gandhinagar Municipal Corporation.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for GSSSB Sanitary Inspector posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed HSC (or equivalent) and hold either a one-year Sanitary Inspector's Diploma or a one-year Certificate of Health Sanitary Inspectors from an ITI, along with basic computer knowledge and knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 33 years of age as on 15/09/2025, with relaxations available for women, reserved categories, persons with disabilities, and ex-servicemen.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary for GSSSB Sanitary Inspector, Class-3?
faq.answer: Selected candidates receive a fixed pay of ₹26,000 per month for the first five years, after which — subject to satisfactory service — they are placed in the 7th Pay Commission scale of ₹25,000 to ₹81,100 (Level-4).

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee?
faq.answer: ₹500 for unreserved category candidates and ₹400 for women (all categories), SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, PwD, and ex-servicemen candidates. The fee is refunded to those who appear for the exam.

faq.question: Q6. What is the exam pattern?
faq.answer: A single-stage CBRT/OMR MCQ exam with two parts: Part A (Reasoning, Data Interpretation, and Mathematics — 60 marks) and Part B (Constitution, Current Affairs, Language Comprehension, and Subject knowledge — 150 marks), totaling 210 marks over 3 hours, with negative marking of 1/4 mark per wrong answer.

faq.question: Q7. What is the last date to apply online?
faq.answer: The revised last date to apply online is 26 July 2026 (11:59 PM), with the fee payable until 29 July 2026 (11:59 PM).
